column6: While I see a distraught penguin, I can't help but applaud (and envy) the courage to make the (tough) call. One might say it's giving up, while it could also be coming to terms with the situations! So much to interpret, so little to infer.
column12: The Japanese macaque meme taught me this penguin's story, and it made me very sad and helpless, just like global warming is unstoppable. I couldn't imagine how I could have helped it if I had wanted to. It also reminded me of many BBC documentaries where multiple photographers, watching groups of penguins, wept, thinking about how their species would disappear in a few decades. I wanted to accompany that penguin, to walk with it into the Antarctic interior, to see what it was searching for. I was heartbroken, but there was nothing I could do.
